steps.title: Identify the contrast and its consequence
steps.content: The passage explicitly states that boredom “creates an anxious tension in the absence of a place to fix attention” and therefore induces “not somnolence but ______.” The key signal is the contrast: the blank must name the agitated, uneasy state that boredom causes—not sleep or drowsiness.

steps.title: Match the anxious state to the answer
steps.content:
Perturbation means disturbance and agitation—the anxious unease boredom creates rather than sleep.

The other three fail to capture this restlessness: fortitude is unrelated to psychological effect, nonchalance contradicts the tension described, and indolence still implies sluggishness.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
